description: Electronic bowling alley — electronics and mechanism repair.
itemDetails:
Tabletop or floor electronic bowling alley games were common family toys from the 1990s through the 2000s, often sold through department stores and toy chains. Typical construction includes a plastic lane, pin reset mechanism, ball return, and battery-powered score or sound electronics.

Common failures on aged units include corroded battery contacts, seized motors, broken gear trains, and tired wiring to sensors or displays. Market value is mostly nostalgic; working examples with complete parts attract stronger interest.

googleReview.quote: Thank you Grace and your family for fixing my son's electronic bowling alley, you have made a little boy so happy. Definitely recommend your services.
